titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jessica Helfand Scrapbook Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 24, 2020 at 04:38

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jessica Helfand Scrapbook Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jessica Helfand Scrapbook Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Decriptions of individual scrapbooks are derived from a database provided by Jessica Helfand.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Scrapbooks are arranged in the order in which they were received from the donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of scrapbooks collected by Jessica Helfand as research for her book Scrapbooks: an American History (Yale University Press, 2008). Also includes examples of volvelles.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Jessica Helfand is an American designer and author and has been Senior Critic at Yale School of Art since 1994.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook of magazine cut outs. Starts with images of bridges and continues with fashion, houses, children, cars, table settings, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 'Baby' is die cut into cover. Contains a photograph of a small child.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper articles regarding death, circa 1900, pasted into Robinson's shorter course - First Book in Arithmetic: Including oral and written exercises (circa 1880).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lillie A. Marsh Rubenstine was born in 1869 and married in 1890. Before her marriage, she taught school for four years. This book was presented to her by some of her pupils. These albums were also commonly known as 'sentiment' albums and are classified by scholars as part of the same genre as autograph books.

did.unittitle: The Animal Rainy Day Scrap Book, by E.L. & E. T. Shuman (The Reilly & Britton Co., Chicago)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Animal photograph have been pasted in to their appropriate locations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Illustrated catalog of coffins and casket with pasted stories, jokes and aphorisms. Includes a racist 'Sambo' joke, in green.

did.unittitle: School Memories, Delmar Studios school photo album
The Girl Graduate : Her Own Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
'Me, Dreaming. 1926.' After graduation took a job in the offices of the DC Cook Publishing Co in Elgin, IL.

did.unittitle: The Girl Graduate: Her Own Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.

Original Pen & Ink and Watercolor artwork tems from Beaumont High, including a 1926 brochure from the Dedication of the New High School. Lots of autographs of friends of Ruby, many include artwork, some nice poetry. There are some good examples of 1920s High School Sports Memorabilia from the St. Louis area, including a booklet from the 16th Annual Field Day of the St. Louis High School League in 1926, a Track and Field Meet.' Also many good ticket stubs.Ruby saved many Party Napkins in her scrapbook and labeled each with Date and location of the party.There is a lengthy diary section in the book. Also included are newspaper clippings, Ribbons, Bridge Tally Cards and much more.
See 'the evolution of a senior/bouillon' what they ate, the doily/next page napkins ... 'we lost as usual'.

did.unittitle: The Girl Graduate: Her Own Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Contains ribbons, clippings, signatures, church meeting minutes, obituaries, class prophecy, check receipt, stamps, hotel stationery, invitations, name cards, programs, drawings, name tags, poems, easter card, and song lyrics.

Includes obituary clipped in later by the family. Thompson never married, and her book is in mint condition with unbelievable photos of her teachers and her friends. Her handwriting is impeccable. Designed and illustrated by Louise Perrett and Sarah K. Smith

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Reilly & Lee. Various reprints taken from his radio show. Three different editions of this book: 1931, 1932-1933, and 1936-1937.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Lined notebook.
Contains clippings from newspapers and magazines, images of movie stars, invitations, lists of 'Books I Have Read' and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.
Contains photographs, pressed flowers, ticket stubs, ribbons, silhouetted cutoutts and items relating to Powell University Training School in Dallas, Texas.

did.unittitle: Animal of the World - Split Page Novelty Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handmade oilcloth book with people and animals. Pages are split to position different heads on different bodies.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Contains photos, invitations, ticket stubs, napkins, valentine, watercolors, calling cards, postcards, dance cards, pencils, charcoal, kitty charm, and autographs.

did.unittitle: The Voice of Kansas : W.I.B.W. 580 Kcs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: School penmanship books used as scrapbook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains news clippings, envelopes, greeting cards, drawings, invitations, photographs, announcements, valentines, calling cards, attendance certificates, membership cards, peppermints package, signatures, paper hat, programs, dried flowers, place cards, menu, napkins, ticket stubs, paper samples, paper plates, prayer card, ribbons, score cards and fabric samples.
Courtesy Project B Collection (Barbara Levine)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Book is bound into zippered case. Unused.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books. School Books.

GRADUATION MEMORIES OF HAZEL. The title page says: LIFE'S FIRST MAJOR TASK COMPLETED. EVENTS THAT WILL NEVER FADE FROM THE MEMORY OF "Hazel Caroline Wiggins." The name was filled in by the owner of the book. She graduated June 12, 1930 and two little snippets of blue and silver fabric from her graduation gown were saved. She graduated from Duluth Central High School, Duluth, Minnesota. Tucked in the book are her graduation announcement, the program from the commencement, the program from "Class Night," and the bulletin from the preceding Sunday from her church the First Methodist Church from a service dedicated to the new graduates. It seems a bit poignant. She filled out the page listing her gifts and her activities the day of graduation, but no friends or faculty signed and there were no clubs or dances or parties. An example of her entries: On June 8th she returned from the church service and wrote: "On returning home I found no one home so spent most of the day by myself except that I went down to Leif Ericcson [sic] Park in the afternoon and listened to the concert given by Duluth Central's High School Band. One of the numbers was "America." Everyone stood up and it was quite thrilling to see people standing all over the hillside and the band playing. In the evening I studied awhile for examinations, and then sat out in the porch swing with Mrs. Bendickson, one of Newell Bendickson's friends threw a ball through the window next door." The pages are held together but the staples pulled loose from the spine. The book measures 7" x 5 1/4". There are some scratches on the suede as you can see in the picture.

did.unittitle: The Girl Graduate: Her Own Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Contains dance cards, pencils, letters, telegrams, and ticket stubs.

did.unittitle: The Physician's Perfect Call List and Record

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Leather bound book with calendar, medical info and more.

did.unittitle: Every Birthday Card I Ever Got - scrapbook
New York In All Its Glory - scrapbook

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various newspaper clippings.

did.unittitle: High School Days: A Book of Record

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Sketch of the school; teachers; photographs; course taken first year; reports first year; sports first year; class entertainments, fairs, dances first year; class gossip first year; chums first year; vacations first year; miscellaneous notes first year; course taken second year; reports second year; sports second year;class entertainments, fairs, dances second year; (etc. thru 4th year);supplementary reading; graduation; class officers; class colors; autographs; clippings; supplementary notes.
Decorations & illustrations by L.J. Bridgeman.Published by H.M. Caldwell and Co., New York and Boston.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
THE McMILLIN-FOLEY PUBLISHING CO

did.unittitle: My Golden School Days: A Record Book for Happy Memories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

My Class, My Classmates, Claff Officers, The Teachers, My Studies, Sports and Athletics, Stunts, Doings, and Jokes, Spreads and Entertainments, Dances, Proms, and Hops, Holidays, Kodak Snap-Shots, Graduation Day, Invitations, Programs, and Clippings, Miscellaneous.

Published by Reilly and Britton ' 1911. Compiled and Arranged by Janet Madison 93 thick pages, 5 1/4" - 7 3/4" tall. Gilt lettering on green spine; moire patterned beige cover with gilt, green & red letters & decoration. First Edition presumably.

did.unittitle: My Memory Book: Chicago World's Fair

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

8 1/2" x 5 1/2" soft cover booklet, the inside pages have the different exhibts with space below to write "Things I Saw", this is unused, some staining on the back cover. Compare with Pittsburgh 'Memory lane' book; Sports 'Mem' book; Motoring Memories; Mother's Memory Book Commencement Memories;

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Vintage Sports Memory Book for high school sports.' 1936--unused except for name in front--no records listed, no scores, no autographs, etc.' 24 pages.' Short articles from college coaches of the time period (Joe Wood--Yale baseball, Coach R.L. Templeton--Stanford track, Matt Mann--Michigan swimming, Lou Little--Columbia University football).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Contains ribbons, clippings, signatures, church meeting minutes, obituaries, class prophecy, check receipt, stamps, hotel stationery, invitations, name cards, programs, drawings, name tags, poems, easter card, and song lyrics. Unused.
Published by Reilly + Lee Designed by H.E. McDonald Arranged by Jan et Madison

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Hey! Hey This is My Book ; Here's a Picture of My School; Class Yearnings (3 Pages); Slick Sayings and Who Said 'Em; My Chums and Why; The Boyfriends and How; All About Me ; My Favorite on the Faculty; Action! My Pet Sports Athletics - My Hero!; Teams & Squads; Games - Meets - Matches; Yells and Cheers; Introducing Our Campus Celebrities; Debates; Laughs; Twosomes; Rushes and Crushes; Frats - Sororities - Clubs; Weekends; Cute Snaps - Pictures of People 'N' Everything (24 Pages for pictures); Endurance Sitters
Copyright by C. R. Gibson & Company Book was designed and illustrated by M. Farini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Contains pages for stunts and frolics; social events - theatres entertainments; amateur theatricals; athletics (basketball, football, track events, baseball, riding, swimming, tennis, golf); my friends (name, birthday anniversary, home, remarks); iary (january-december); snapshots; miscellaneous.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Dixie Lids scrapbook. IMAGE FIVE is an alternative -- not in our collection. issued'as a cover for the premium photos of movie stars that you could get from'sending in your Ice Cream Dixie lids. cutting an

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

The book has sections to complete with info for: School Pennants, School Cheer, School Songs, Autographs, Teachers, Exam Marks, Sports Teams, Scandal!, Commencement, Graduation Presents and more. Some of the pages have newspaper clipping, pages from exams, Graduation Program, School Song Program, a pencil drawing of a teacher, hand written information re school colors, song, sheers, sports team names, etc.
Designed by Amelia Winter

Unused
Illustrated by Edna Mason Kaula. Published by Richard G. Krueger, Inc. New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dearest of darling creatures that walk or crawl ... it is for you my heart throbs so violently at night, that the house is shaken to its very foundations ... Percival Inglesides School New Milford

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 volumes. Black and white images of Hollywood actresses and actors. Includes loose post cards of same.

did.unittitle: My Commencement: Memories and Records of School Life

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
NY: Dodd, Mead and Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inside are mementos of Janet and Curtis's skating career including those great fuzzy patches with their names under winged skates, several photographs, a post card, match pack as well as patches and labels similar to travel labels from different roller rinks. One from Russiaville, Indiana ROLL A WAY SKATING RINK shows a girl skating with a soldier and a sailor and reads For Health's Sake ' Roller Skate and For Victory ' Buy Bonds so this must be from the 40s and WWII era. Shafer Lake, Chesterton ' Indianapolis & Frankfort Indiana, Washington D.C., and New York City ('Gay Blades') are all represented'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Cards and tallies from three bridge parties in 1929, an envelope, placecard, postcard and toothpick (still in wrapper!) from the Post Tavern in Battle Creek Michigan 1929, a play bill from the Apollo Theatre in Chicago...play was "Follow Thru" Laurence Shwab and Frank Mandel, play began in August 1929. My favorite: a Western Union telegram dated August 22, 1929 from Lawrence to Eleanor speaking of a weekend at the cottage!
Green cover - Book published by W.C. Horn and Co., Newark, NJ. Arranged by Elizabeth M. Owen and illustrated by Bernice Hunt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Purple cover - empty and in original box Book published by W.C. Horn and Co., Newark, NJ. Arranged by Elizabeth M. Owen and illustrated by Bernice Hunt.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused

Classmates Autographs; Favprote Studies, Athletics, School and Social Functions, Clubs and Societies, Colors, Motto, Class Yell, Rushing Parties, Officers and Members, Entertainments, Lectures, Plays, Glee Clubs, Favorite Professors, Memorable Trips, Clippings, Photographs, Class Prophecy, Baccalaureate Sermon, Gifts, Miscellaneous.
Barse & Hopkins, New York, 1914

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memory Books.
Unused
Pages for memories includes my class, class song, classmates autographs, the faculty, societies and clubs, favorite studies and athletics and many more other subjects.
Arranged by Elizabeth F. Boynton Ilustrations by J.T. Armbrust Published by Jordan & Company Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused
C.R. Gibson & Company, NYC

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.
Photos annotated with marriage and divorce statistics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Page headings include: Naughty biography, Feelings, The People's Choice, Our Warmest Friend (the janitor), Principal's page, My Dear Teachers, Parents page, Heart Throbs, Friends predictions, Enemies, Sports Forecast, Sports Calendar, My Lie Detector Test, Nominations, Hot Sketches, Cartoon records, Codes, Movie Passions, Our Jives, Penpals, and black sheets for signatures.

Covers by Harold M. Sherman, cartoons by Charles Forbell, Charles Forbell was born in Brooklyn, New York, and attended the Pratt Institute, before joining the art staff of the New York World. Around 1910, he began contributing cartoons to magazines like Life and Judge, doing cartoon series like 'In Ye Goode Old Days' (Life) and 'In Ancient Times' and 'Ancient Sources of Modern Inventions' (Judge) throughout the 1910s, 1920s and 1930s. He also created the shortlived newspaper comic 'Naughty Pete' in 1913. American Stationery Products, Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused
C.R. Gibson & Co., Norwalk, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused
Shrewesbury Publishing, Chicago

Unused

did.unittitle: Happy School Days: A Memory Book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Headings include: my class, year, colors, pin or ring; my schoolmates, their autographs, photographs and nicknames; my favorite teachers, their autographs and photographs; My chum and other best friends; favorite studies; favorite books and authors; parties; outings; entertainments; class plays; athletics; kodak snap shots; commencement (class day, class history and prophecy), reunions and miscellaneous.
Book copyright 1918 Reilly and Britton Co

Contains photographs and biographical notes collected by the parents of Wilbur Russell Yates. Also contains two locks of Wilbur Russell Yates' baby hair.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Babyhood Days by Jessie Alma Pierson, frontispiece by Evelyn von Hartmann. Published in New York by Barse and Hopkins. Unused.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For Edmund Patterson

Contains animal fur, summaries, narrations, school work, photos, report cards, newspaper comics, animal clippings, watercolors, phonics workbook, sheet music, envelopes, wedding invitations, commencement invitations, notes, school tablet, high school yearbook, diagram, birth announcement
Scrapbook made by two aunts for their nephew, Edmund Patterson (1906-2008), all about their brother/his father, Edmund Dewitt Patterson (1876-1953).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Wedding Books.
Jordan and Co. Chicago by Adelaide S. Jordan. In original box.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused
Barse and Hopkins, 1922. Illustrated by Margaret Marion Coughlin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Bridal Books.
Contains photos, letters, clippings, pressed flowers, dried ferns from bouquet, 4 pages of wedding gifts, fabric samples from bride's gown,

Designed by Cornelia Morton Weyburn. Published by Barse and Hopkins, New York, 1912. Records Marietta, GA society wedding of Christine Dobbs to George Nichols. Childhood sweethearts. Bride's father was a state senator from GA (Democrat) -- looks like he may have been mayor of marietta they were married june 1 1916 later moved to bronxville. George died in 1995. George G. Nichols Jr. '40 George Nichols died Dec. 17, 1995, at his home in Bronxville, N.Y., where he had been a lifelong resident. Coming to Princeton from Bronxville H.S., George majored in English and roomed with Ed Bennett. Following graduation he pursued studies toward entering medical school which were interrupted by WWII. George joined the Navy, became a line officer rising to the rank of lieutenant. For four years he served on an attack transport and participated in the invasions of North Africa and Okinawa. Following the war, George joined the reinsurance brokerage firm of Guy Carpenter, Inc. In 1963 he was elected chief executive of the firm and retired as chairman of the executive committee in 1980. Under his stewardship, Guy Carpenter experienced enormous growth and expansion to become one of the largest of its kind in the world. From 1973-78 George was also a senior v.p. and director of Marsh & McLennan Companies, Inc. George is survived by his sisters Margaret N. Allport and Christine N. Tredway. To them both we extend our heartfelt sympathy and condolences. The Class of 1940

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Bridal Books.
Contains clippings of advertisements and pictures, pasted into the scrapbook. Includes images of body builders,babies, and toilet seats.
Wedding scrapbook, given to Rose and Bill [Coprowski?].

did.unittitle: All About You: An Adopted Child's Memory Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.
Unused
Marks of Identification; first haircut; bright doings and sayings; 'if from abroad' and more.
The first record book designed especially for adopted children. By MacLeod, Marion with illustrations by Helen Berry Moore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Monograms, addresses, logos and photographs. The Kewpie doll on the inside cover is great!! It shows rooms, schoolmates, school plays, horse and buggy busses, lots of costumes and dressing up, staff, graduates, school trips, campus buildings, May Pole festivities, sporting events, parties, swimming, canoeing, Mandolin Club, graduation, some various "boys", dogs, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused
Includes listings for specific events for up to five children; accompanying poems, songs, space for photos and clippings, etc.
Published by Our Yesterdays, Inc. Houston and Dallas, TX. Compiled by Morgan Sivia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interesting vintage sewing scrapbook with sewing samples and instructions, probably from a sewing class. There are about 15 pages of instructions and samples and another 5 pages of embroidery samples with no instruction. The rest of the pages are blank, but the whole book is in great condition.

Interesting vintage sewing scrapbook with sewing samples and instructions, probably from a sewing class. There are about 15 pages of instructions and samples and another 5 pages of embroidery samples with no instruction. The rest of the pages are blank, but the whole book is in great condition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused

Includes ribbons, clippings, signatures, church meeting minutes, obituaries, class prophecy, check receipt, stamps, hotel stationery, invitations, name cards, programs, drawings, name tags, poems, easter card, and song lyrics.
Published by Barse & Hopkins, NY Designed by Victor de Kubinyi

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains magazine and newspaper clippings, as well as some pasted-in correspondence.

did.unittitle: Loose papers relating to Rodolfo Eladio Acena

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains magazine and newspaper clippings, as well as a high school diploma (1930). Also includes collected biographical material regarding Rodolfo Eladio Acena (circa 1893-1945) and Felicida N. Acena (circa 1893-1979).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains roll calls, logs, awards, hand written chapter history, and photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.

Copyright Givens and Co., Western Springs. Ill. Oversized and unused book with blind embossed stork on the cover and 'Scoopy' and 'Scrappy' bound in as half pages to guide the collector. Themed pages include My Family; Movie Stars; Radio Programmes; Song Hits; even Appliances: Scoopy says, 'With so many different streamlined appliances for household use, it will be easy to collect material for these pages.'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A scrapbook dedicated to Reverend George C. Magill's dog Trix. Contains writings, photographs and newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused, and in original box. Contains a small green card, which has been placed into the book. Arranged by Janet Madison and decorated by E.M. McDonald. Published by The Reilly and Lee Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.

Photos, Dance Cards, Theater Playbills, Grade Reports, Sports Ticket Stubs, Souvenirs, Many personal Letters and Notes, Dried Flowers, Delta Phi Gamma Memorabilia, Freshman Rules, Commencement Papers, A Type Written Play, Real Hair Samples, and much, much more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: D in Psychology C- in dramatics at Temple. Kitty's always late it seems to be her fate so here's a little watch to help her keep her date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photo of Rudolph Valentino, receipts, prayer card, junior prom ticket, ideal figure chart, cardboard key, memo pad, love letters, clippings, Indian pageant program, obituaries for Fritz Freiberg, poems, drawings, place cards, watercolor of a Packard car, play program, instructions for back seat drivers, certificate of proficiency in typewriting, cracker jack riddles, birth announcement, dance cards, crossword, horoscopes, golf score-card, get well soon card, valentine, twig, firecrackers, bridge tally sheets, tuition account book, ticket stubs,rush card, treasure map, English test, itinerary, wooden nickel, menu, postcards, and christmas cards.

A young woman's scrapbook of her high school Hyde Park High and college days at the University of Chicago in the 1920's. Many items are college related and dated 1922-1927. best quote: These are the days of suffragetting, profiteering, rent hogs, excess taxes and prohibition.If you think life is worth living, we wish you A HAPPY NEW YEAR.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Ye Auld Alma Mater; Auld Lang Syne; Ye Auld Self; Candid Shots; Ye Generalissimos (Student Officers, etc.); Ye Announcement (Commencement); Autographs and Random Scribbles; On Our Way
From Wyoming Stationery Company, Casper WY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.

Contains certificates, letters, napkin, photographs, classmate log, invitations, nametags, candy wrapper, train ticket, gift tags, stamps, membership cards, newspaper clippings, envelopes, ribbon, tissue paper, postcards, receipts, Chinese customs declaration form, coupon book, valentine, christmas cards, advertisements, cartoons, playbills, cigar labels, sports schedule, maps, liquor labels, travel brochures, hotel soap labels, menu, music sheets, and 2 issues of needlecraft magazine.

Also includes Standard First Aid Certificate, course given by Oklahoma Gas and Electric; approx. 7 pages of names with address, nickname, and their thoughts; several student activity tickets; numerous event name tags including sorority, fraternity, and organizational functions; several membership cards (Engineering Society, YMCA, etc.); ticket stub to Tulsa football game; several letters with stamped envelopes; Christmas cards and other holiday greetings items; postcard of the "Empress of Asia" ship; customs form for imported merchandise from China; tons of photos including the car "Leaping Leana", football game and crowd, different views of campus/buildings, many individuals, parade photos, etc. ... copy of the booklet "Freshman Bible;" quite a few newspaper clippings; several concert and play programs; a couple of neat football schedules (one booklet and one football shaped); cigar, beer, and candy labels; ticket for Allens' Motor Lines; several commencement programs; Junior-Senior Banquet Booklets; graphic cutout from the souvenir program of the grand opening of the Ritz in Muskogee; neat Greyhound Lines map; timetables from the Pacific Greyhound Lines; hat checks from the Southern Kansas Stage Lines; bus timetable and map of the Santa Fe Trail Limited; booklet on Historic Old New Orleans; numerous items from hotels in several cities; large newspaper photos and articles of Rex's arrival in the Mardi Gras Parade; etc.

did.unittitle: Book for Scraps : History of House Built by Dr. & Mrs. Shoemaker

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Only a small bit of information on the house; then some newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: F.W. Woolworth blank pre-gummed pages for easy scrapbooking!

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.

Contains Serenade, Halowe'en, Games, Field Day, Interclass Games, Interclass Debates, Class Ballot-Girl; Clasws Ballot-Man, Class History, Class Poem, Class Prophecy, My Graduation, Addresses, Gifts, Congratulations, Programs, Invitations, Clippings, Snap Shots, Stunts, Stories, Chafing Dish Recipes, Candy Recipes, Other Recipes, Vacations, Holidays, Excursions, Houser Parties, Reunions, Picnics, and Miscellaneous. Also includes photogtraphs, congratulatory cards, letters, American Red Cross letter, clippings, programs, and ribbons.
Illustrated by J.T. Armbrust. Published by Jordan Co., Chicago Arranged by Elizabeth Furlong.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.

Born; Photographs; Autographs; Baby's First Picture; The Baby's Basket (includes space for 'a bit of the basket's drapery'); Congratulations on the Baby's Coming; Gifts for the Baby; 'The Baby Was Carried Upstairs' (there is an old superstition that a baby should be carried upstairs, that it may rise in the world, before it is taken downstairs); First Creeping; Baby's First Journey, Chronicled by the Baby's Mother; Extraordinary Things Baby Did; Some of Baby's Own Ways.
Published 1915. Illustrations by Meta Morris Grimbali

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.

Born; Photographs; Autographs; Baby's First Picture; The Baby's Basket (includes space for 'a bit of the basket's drapery'); Congratulations on the Baby's Coming; Gifts for the Baby; 'The Baby Was Carried Upstairs' (there is an old superstition that a baby should be carried upstairs, that it may rise in the world, before it is taken downstairs); First Creeping; Baby's First Journey, Chronicled by the Baby's Mother; Extraordinary Things Baby Did; Some of Baby's Own Ways.
Published 1924 by The Saalfield Publishing Company, Decorated by Josephine Wheeler Wage.

did.unittitle: The Dietary Adventures of Anabil Lee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.
Space for Baptism details, weight and height chart, reception committee, baby's foot, thumb and hand print, teeth, and pages for photographs. See Mother's entries opposite under 'Current Opinion'

Thomas was Instructor in Dietetics at University of Minnesota, and her book was an attempt at using the food experiences of the fictional Anabil 'to present the food problems of the young mother in the order in which they arrive.' Published 1924.
Baby was David Allen Hecker.

did.unittitle: The Dietary Adventures of Anabil Lee

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.
Space for Baptism details, weight and height chart, reception committee, baby's foot, thumb and hand print, teeth, and pages for photographs. See Mother's entries opposite under 'Current Opinion'

Thomas was Instructor in Dietetics at University of Minnesota, and her book was an attempt at using the food experiences of the fictional Anabil 'to present the food problems of the young mother in the order in which they arrive.' Published 1924.

Baby was Helen Alice Crane.
Under 'Current Opinion' 'The healthiest baby ... that solid flesh ... how big ... so friendly. How'd you get those ears so sail boaty? Chubby, cupid hands. Beautiful hair, another curly head -- a brunette. How she can eat! Don't you keep her too tightly clothed, mother? Slower than sister Carolyn; just as smart as sister. Nice mouth; good head. Chinese eyes. Those ears! What a good baby.'

did.unittitle: Baby Grows in Age and Grace: A Guide and Record for the Catholic Mother.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.

Chapters: Motherhood as co-operation with God; Signs of development (Physical, Spiritual); Record of Baptism; World Happenings on Baby's Birthday; Preventative Measures for Illness (Diptheria, Whooping Cough, Scarlet Fever); Favorite Spontaneous Prayer, Hymn; Development signs -- recognized these holy pictures; Attendance at First Mass
Published by CR Gibson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Emery Boook Company, Springfield, NJ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.

Opening quote: 'Who hath not saved some trifling thing, More prized than jewels rare, A faded flower, a broken ring, a tress of golden hair.' (poem by Ellen C. Howarth, aka Clementine) Only illustrations -- no headings -- but intro says they are for 'ilnesses, injuries, response to rhythm and music, creative tendencies, special aptitudes, etc.

Record book published by The Mothers Aid/Chicago Lying-In Hospital. 'In presenting the delightfully illustrated Scrapbook, we make possible an orderly collection of those priceless mementoes that too frequently are lost or damaged beyond repair. A small cardboard box, yellow with age, may serve very well as a movie prop but it is a sorry container for the precious and irreplacable leap to life once again, reminders that make up the color and patterns of a child's life. Cherished memories of the past are recalled and live again as you turn the pages of this Scrapbook. Each event, insignificant or important, recedes into forgetfulness until the Scrapbook is opened ant there, before you, your memories.'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Christmas cards, stamps, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is an interesting hand done scrapbook of the Western Penman penmanship samples. The hardbound book measures 12 inches by 10 inches and is filled with pages from the various samples and lessons of calligraphy / penmanship of the 1800s. Each lesson and sample was carefully pasted onto the pages of this scrapbook, approximately 30 pages. The front cover has a hand printed label Penman Journal and Western Penman 1877 Scrapbook. There is also a name inscribed (in careful script): E.H. Campbell 485 Linden St. Fall River, Mass. This is an interesting example of the customs and educational practices of the 1800s.

did.unittitle: Commenced Collection of Monograms, Crests and Trademarks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains cutouts, monograms, cyphers, crests, advertizing art, trademarks, decorative borders, cartouches, typography sample alphabets, slogans, decorative initials, and Hebrew.

did.unittitle: Company Coming : A Handy Guide for the Hostess

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused

A personal guide to your guests and their tastes, and tips on entertaining, hints on mixing perfect drinks, a guide to wines and their service, table settings and seating arrangements. Distributed by National Blank book Co, Holyoke Massachusetts. Published by Hawthorn Books, NYC. Book is complete with it's original box.

did.unittitle: The Mary Schultz Home Study Scrapbooks

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains lesson notes, scraps, and pieces of fabric with stains accompanied by instructions for their removal.

did.unittitle: My Golden School Days ~ Class Memories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

My Class; My Classmates; Class Officers; The Teachers; My Studies; Sports and Athletics; Stunts, Doings and Jokes; Spreads and Entertainments; Dances, Proms and Hops; Holidays; Kodak Snap-Shots; graduation Day; Invitations, Programs and Clippings; Miscellaneous.
Great autographs, including: When you get married and your husband gets cross, come to my house and make applesauce.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Unused
Gibson & Co. Norwalk CT

did.unittitle: School Day Treasures of Happy Memories

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused
The World Syndicate Publishing Company of Cleveland and New York.

did.unittitle: School Days: My Book of Remembrance

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Contains election of officers; autographs; looking ahead; sports enthusiasms; school play; extra curricular activities.
Published by CR Gibson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bronze-tone embossed cover in original box.
Belonged to Edward Holcomb.

did.unittitle: A Girl's Graduation Days - scrapbook and loose papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: I purchased this book from an estate sale in Natchitoches inn the early 1980's. Lydia Blanchard was the class historian and also a very accomplished artist, as can be evidenced by her artwork in this book. It includes many drawings, articles, letters, autographs, cartoons, photographs, ephemera (they played a lot of bridge so there are some wonderful tallies) and writings and views of an 18 year old girl at the time of her graduation and entry into the world.

Lydia Blanchard graduated from St. Mary's Academy in Natchitoches, LA. She talks about the parties they had, the food they ate and just everyday thoughts of a young girl. One page she talks about "Eskimo Pies" and how much they loved them. You can tell it was a very important and exciting time for her. There was also some loose papers, articles, letters and other miscellaneous items in the box the book came in. One interesting item in this lot is a Souvenir Ticket stating that Lydia had traveled by automobile over the Pikes Peak Auto Highway to the summit of Pikes Peak, dated Aug. 20, 1922.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

My class; year; yell; motto; colors; flower; description of class pin; my classmates; their autographs and Photographs; class officers; the faculty; my favorite teachers; their autographs and photographs; favorite studies; societies; the glee club; the dramatic club; spreads and entertainments; stunts & jokes; proms-dances-hops; sports & athletics; the teams; clippings & press notices; rushes & mass meetings; invitations & programs; holidays; reunions; kodak snap-shots; commencement; class history; class prophecy; class poem; baccalaureate sermon; and miscellaneous.

Built in 1910 the high school opened just as the formerly quiet hamlet of Hollywood was first being overrun by movie producers. By the 20s, it became the school of choice for the children of movie stars. But as the neighborhood grew more seedy with the passing years, and with a change in school district boundaries, most of the well-to-do Hollywood parents stopped sending their children to Hollywood High, opting instead for schools like Beverly Hills High and Santa Monica High.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Hollywood High School. Includes autographs, photographs, newspaper clippings, theatre programs, placecards, party invitations, ticket stubs, cigarette stubs, and song lyrics.
Reilly & Britton 1927

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.

Many photos (including lighthouses), handwritten diary entries, menus, drawings, programs, movie advertisements, postcards (including real photos), greeting cards, tally cards, interesting looking postage stamps, letters, initiation items, etc. She wrote of typical Adolph Menjouul teenage things such as parties, boys (she had a serious boyfriend named Hector towards the end of her time at school), trips, events, etc.
2 scrapbooks kept by Verna Leonard of Brockton, Massachusetts, who boarded at Hyannis Normal School (becoming Hyannis Teachers College while she was there).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: School Books.

Many photos (including lighthouses), handwritten diary entries, menus, drawings, programs, movie advertisements, postcards (including real photos), greeting cards, tally cards, interesting looking postage stamps, letters, initiation items, etc. She wrote of typical Adolph Menjouul teenage things such as parties, boys (she had a serious boyfriend named Hector towards the end of her time at school), trips, events, etc.
2 scrapbooks kept by Verna Leonard of Brockton, Massachusetts, who boarded at Hyannis Normal School (becoming Hyannis Teachers College while she was there).

did.unittitle: Baby's Kingdom: Whereupon may be chronicled as memories for grown up days.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.
1890 by Lee and Shepard. Designed and illustrated by Annie F. Cox, author of 'The Guest Book.' Love's stories, written in love's richest book.

All about Baby, her eating habits ("milk from Gilberts Farm paid ten cents a quart",) Baby Announcement, first illness, first baby carriage ride, small 4th July flag waved, photos of other babies, mama & baby photo, beautiful antique Greeting Cards (Valentines), Christmas's from the beginning & other holidays, mention of 2nd baby Ruth born 1896, first tooth, photos Mama & girls at beach, photos Papa & Pauline, Grandma, dressing & outfits described, baby photos (great one 2 babies in parasol-covered pram), first words, other cousins, First Birthday! many details, other babies birthdays noted & announcement, July 1898 3 weeks spent at Snow Inn, Cape Cod, locks of sister Ruth's hair, photo of Ruth, notes written to Pauline as child, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Shrewsbury Pub Co Chicago Illustrated by Cornelia M. Weyburn.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

The Faculty; My Studies; My Class Motto; Our Class Colors; My Class Yell; Class Officers; Blank Page with opposite page entitled Photographs; Autograph/Addresses; Societies; Music; Athletics; Tennis; Feasts and Festivities; Feasts; Spreads; Feasts Fun & Frolics; Fun Frolics; Dramatics; Invitations; Programs; Blank; Social Events and Entertainments; Social Events; Entertainments; Class Poem; Class History; Blank Page; Press Notices; Clippings; Class Prophecy; Baccalaureate Sermon; Blank Page; Class Day; Gifts; Notes.

MEMORIES OF MY SCHOOL DAYS by Elise E. Edwards with pages to be filled in by student. Hardcover. 6 1/2" x 9". On front free endpaper the following is written "Best Wishes and Luck, Mr. S. Robb Howell From Mr. & Mrs. Warden Kyes, May 29, 1925.

1956. J. N. Rasbach Photo Serivce, Inc.

Introduction to Society; Congratulations; Luncheons; Dinners; Teas; Theatre; Opera; Card Parties; Dances; Weddings; Holidays; Week End Trips; Camera; Press Notices; Miscellaneous.
Barse + Hopkins 1916 designed by Evelyn von Hartmann

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unused
10x14 inches with leather lace binding.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New York: Grenberg Publisher, 1947.
Compiled by Helen H. Thurber with Decorations by Florence Daly.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Gasoline and Oil Records; Goodrich Touring Bureau Pages 16-17; Interchangeable Tires Page 3; Road Loc of an Interesting Trip Pages; Tire Don'ts Page; Tire Record Pages; What to Take on Your Trip; To Store Tires - inside back cover; Goodrich Service Stores - back cover.

Note there are two versions. 'There is much genuine pleasure in recalling delightful days spent in your car. In this little book keep a record of your jaunts ' it will be an unending source of pleasure and may often be of value.' Published by the BF Goodrich Company.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Red book includes pages for childhood hims, school hims (dreamboats, goons), special hims (date bait, charmers and gay deceivers), popular hims, national hims, musical hims, instructive hims, sentimental hims, celebrated hims, older hims, VIPs, and finally, the 'him of hims.'
Red book has black silhouettes and fill-in lines for where seen (the stalker version). Second book has a wood cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Emery Book Company, Springfield, NJ. "Some things are BULKY, And some things won't PASTE - So I'll shove 'em in HERE, And they won't go to WASTE."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes many real photographs of soldiers and General John J. Pershing. It has real photo pocards of the Spanish American War as well. Contains many personal articles, including letters home to her mothers, a Pershing family re-union with the General as the speaker, lots of postcards, real newspapers laid in, small flags in envelopes, and hundreds of articles on the general as well as the war in general.

Book created by a niece of General Black Jack Pershing, Jesse Pershing of Toledo, Ohio. Her father fought with Black Jack Pershing in the Spanish American War. At that time there were 19 members of Pershing's family serving in the military. Her brother was inthe war with the general as well. She was a photographer and took photos of camps, soldiers, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Candy and sweets labels: Little Boy Blue, E.B. Petter Shipper, Leesburg, Fla; Hickcok's Nut Butter Kisses; Necco Wafers; Penn-Mar Chocolate Coated Caramels; Badger's Milwaukee Distinctive Sweets; Hershey's "More Sustaining than Meat"; Hunny Munch; Get Happy; Cretors' Tosty Rosty Delicious Pop Corn; Baby Ruth The 5c Bar; Beech-nut Brand Chewing Gum Mint Flavored Always Refreshing; Clarks Teaberry Quality Gum; Collins Honey Scotch; Planters See-Thru Virginia Bar; Love Nest 5c Milk Chocolate The Euclid Candy Co of New York, Inc.; Hershey's Mint Flavor Chewing Gum; Puritan Googie Juniors Chocolate Peanut; Cap'N Pat the Chocolate Mint Patty 5c; Planters 5c Nut Confections; Crest Chocolates Assorted Thanksgiving Greetings, Walter M. Lowney Co; Johnston's Chocolate Nougat Bar; Banta's Shimme Waffle; Oh, Johnnie! Milk Nut Roll Ucanco Candy Co., Davenport, IA; Hickok's Whippoorwill; Oh MaMa Pine Appa Roll Milk Chocolate; and many more.

Some of the wrappers: Belle of Florida Brand A.S. Herlong & Co Leesburg, Fla, Shippers of Fancy Citrus Fruits; Queens Taste, W.P.Peyton Fruit Co., Los Angeles, CA; Nunnally's The Candy of the South (Box label and individual candy wrapper); Cox Theater Program with advertising for Morning Glory Brand Hams & Bacon, Icy-Hot Gallon Vacuum Jar etc; calling cards and notes (such as "Gentleman to see you 2:30" and "Dear Marybelle. at your convenience would you let me have the dollar you owe me for the coaching lesson before xmas. It's embarrassing to have to ask but I need the money and you probably have forgotten about it. Sincerely-Grace").

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fabric swatches, photographs, dance cards, stunt cards, place cards, news clippings, calling cards, school programs, invitations, postcards, telegrams, letters, pressed flower, 1918 visitor's pass to Camp Kearny, calling card for Mrs. Cecil Blount de Mille, school rules and dress code.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook dated from 1935-37. Includes camp memoribilia from Missouri Montebello. Contains photographs and newpaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Note: 2 different copies

Fourteen full-size linen-like pages of drawings, clippings and handwritten poetry; it is a record of the relationship betwen Peter and Polly; some of the pages have water marks; on one page is a clipping of a young woman, college age, wearing a letter sweater with "07" on it indicating 1907 and there is no place I can see where a date is mentioned; in addition there is a partial school essay on American Poets; there is also a program from 1919 from the "Wieting Opera House", which I believe is located on the Syracuse University campus-the program is for Booth Tarkington's "Seventeen"; this scrap book came from a collection of Alexandria Bay, 1000 Islands items, all from the Delany and Visger estates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Black book, black pages. Full of family photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.

Staunton Military Academy in Staunton, Virginia. Staunton Military Academy was a well regarded school for young men and was started in 1860, just before the Civil War and the school closed in 1976. This scrapbook belonged to a Richard S. Day and he was a Cadet at this school circa 1921. He'was from Scranton, Pennsylvania.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The material includes various invitations, dance programs, auditorium programs including minstrel shows, photos, newspaper clippings, and other keepsakes including pressed flowers, cigarettes, and a stick of chewing gum.

Ellen Amelia Row (1892-1982). Ms Row was a graduate of Clearfield H. S. in 1911 and in 1914, a graduate of the 4 year course at the Indiana Pennsylvania State Normal School. The scrapbook measures 10 1/4 by 12 1/4 inches. The scrapbook, manufactured by the Samuel Ward Co. of Boston, contains 30 double-sided scrapbook sheets. The majority of sheets and pages in the scrapbook were never used. There are 13 pages with artifacts attached to them, though there are also some loose clippings. The majority relate to Ms Row's graduation from the State Normal School (now Indiana University of Pennsylvania) while others pertain to Clearfield H. S. and the Kiskiminetas Springs School. Ms. Row married D. Earl Shirey of her hometown, Clearfield, PA in 1918. One of the inserts in the scrapbook is a typed record on Ed L. Shirey & Son letterhead that documents Earl's service in WWI, their engagement and marriage. Online computer census information for 1930 show Earl as a manager of wholesale lumber in Clearfield and the couple raising a son, Edwin Earl, age 10.

did.unittitle: Camp Andree Clark Girl Scout Album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The base of the scrapbook is a "composition" notebook, of which there is normal journal like writings in it. There are also loose letters on the camps letterhead, a sign handmade form the "wash house", a sign about flowers, magazine clippings, two green certificates, some hand drawings of merit patches, and more.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books. School Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings, pictures, tickers, souvenirs, and drawings. Includes divorce stories.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This was assembled in the middle of the Great Depression, so it's all been quite artfully put in a Large old Book, probably to save buying a new 'empty Scrapbook.' The Book is "The History of Banking and Banks" and is dated 1884. The book was put together by a Fanatical Radio Fan in Portland, Oregon. There's a article about Mel Blanc, who got his start on KEX Radio in Portland, before he went on to voice most of Warner Brothers' cartoon characters.
Made from 'The History of Banking and Banks' 1884

did.unittitle: The Modern Baby Book and Child Development Record

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.
Illustrated by Clara Elsene Peck.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs from an album found in an antique store in Kansas City, all taken between 1917 and 1932.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: American girl in French convent -- made scrapbook of handwork (sewing, mending) with notes in French and exquisite typography.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook of movie stars and Lindbergh Kidnapping.

did.unittitle: My College Record: A Memory Book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Chicago: Reilly & Britton Illustrated by Clara Powers Wilson

Illustrations by Mabel Betsy Hill '1921 C.R. Gibson and Co. blank, boxed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
School Days: A Memory Book" by Josephine Bruce, copyright 1907. The name written in the front of this book is "Kathryn Adams, Friends School, Baltimore, Maryland."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.

On My Way Home Again; Military Camps of the Nation; This is Me (photos); Regular Fellows I Won't Forget; High Lights and Furloughs; I Advance-Win Citations; A Chronology of World War ll; My Favorite Pin-ups; All over the Map Pictorial Record of World War l; Scrap Book; Maps- 62 pages

"The G-I's own commemorative record of his gallant and victorious achievements in World War 11.....in whatever branch he served.... Army-Navy-Marines. World-Wide Maps.... Official Army-Navy Photographs."; this blurb on the tattered dust jacket of this unused personal Record book of the returning Vet of the "Great War".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.

Blank book published 1942 by The Hickory Publishing Co., Inc. 'This book is published for the purpose of being filled in by a relative or a friend of a man who is serving his country. When it is completed, it will serve as a record of his experiences and observations during the war which the United States of America entered on December 8th, 1941.'

did.unittitle: Life in The Service: Echoes of the Army

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.
General Advertising Publishers, Inc. Chicago

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.
1942 Stationers Specialty Corp, NYC

did.unittitle: My Life in The Service: Echoes of the Army

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.
It is a nice convenient size for fitting into a "Class A" Tunic pocket. It measures 6" x 4 1/2" and is over 200 pages long.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.
Given out as a promotional item by a bank.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.
'By The Men in the Service'

Anthology published 1943 by Industrial Tape Corp. 'During the last year, we had no Texcel Cellophane Tape for general distribution, and probably won't have for some time to come. Our present production is devoted to the war effort. But we didn't want our millions of friends throughout the United States to forget us. We wanted to keep reminding them that Texcel Tape would be back, after the war. And may we hope that this 'Scrapbook' will help you remember Texcel pleasantly, when it is again available.'

did.unittitle: WWII Air Force Scrapbook: My Life in The Forces

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Army Books.

"My First Photograph in Uniform" and "My Best Girl's Photograph"; "Records and Notes"; "Autographs"; "Personal Service Records"; "Names and Addresses of Family and Friends"; "Names of Officers"; "Birthdays and Occasions I Must Not Forget"; "Physical Record"; "Gifts Received While in the Service"; "Foreign Places and Countries I Visited" and "Discharged from the Service"

"We have purposely omitted numerous sub-headings on the white sheets to enable you to enter your own data, as the occasion may require--such as promotions, transfers, furloughs, maneuvers, athletics, range records, etc. / We suggest keeping SNAPS and SCRAPS in the mailing box--it will not only keep it clean but can be used for remailing if you should desire to send 'SNAPS and SCRAPS' home for your parents, wife or sweetheart to keep up to date for you."

"Snaps and Scraps has been developed in accordance with personal views and preferences of men in the services. They expressed a section of white paper, suitable for writing with ink, on which they could record their military life. The most decided preferfence was for a pocket to file the clippings, photos, letters from home and miscellaneous material, until ready to mount in the SNAP or SCRAP sections, or otherwise to be disposed of. / The flexible cover permits rolling of the entire book if so desired. The size is not large enough to be cumbersome in the limited storage space available to service men."

did.unittitle: A Memory Book of My School Days

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Class, Year, Colors, Pin or Ring; Schoolmates, Their Autographs, Photographs and Nicknames; Teachers; My Chum and Other Best Friends; Favorite Studies; Favorite Books and Authors; Parties; Outings; Entertainments; Class Plays; Athletics; Kodak Snap Shots; Commencement; Class Day, History, Prophecy; Reunions; Miscellaneous.
Published 1920 by The Reilly & Lee Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged and designed by Helen A. Haselton Published by Reilly and Lee.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Class, Year, Colors, Pin or Ring; Schoolmates, Their Autographs, Photographs and Nicknames; Teachers; My Chum and Other Best Friends; Favorite Studies; Favorite Books and Authors; Parties; Outings; Entertainments; Class Plays; Athletics; Kodak Snap Shots; Commencement; Class Day, History, Prophecy; Reunions; Miscellaneous.
Published 1920 by The Reilly & Lee Co. Illustrations by Florence White Williams.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.

Also includes a few paper items found in the book: 2 Programs for the Junior-Senior Reception held at the K. of P. Hall, Monday, May 22, 1922. Names included are: Marjorie Baker, Irene Sheets, and Louise Miller. Also included is a program from the Junior- Senior Reception K of P. Hall Friday, May 13, 1921. Names included are: Rex Settlemire, Richard Ruck, Rosamond Esser, Harry Barber, Charles Knost, Kurt Mueller, Clarence McGoff, and Edward Musser. Finally there is an Ohio Pupils Reading Circle Certificate for Roy Miller. Printed on the card is the year 1922.
Designed and Illustrated by Claire Powers Wilson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.

Published as a promotion for new mothers by Libby's. Mary Hale Martin was a nutritionist for Libby, McNeil and Libby Great illustrations. Immunization record (whooping cough, etc) and an ad for Libby's spinach on the last page

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Memory Books.

Me and my shadow; faculty; advice to the lovelorn; among mortals; random scribbles; 'mes ami' and 'meine freunde' and 'i miei amici' and 'mis amigos' ('my friends in any language') with spaces for name, best point, hobby, pet peeve and, 'quote'; I knew him when; and red letter days.
"We go our way, the world to buck, please sign within and wish us luck."

did.unittitle: From Day To Day At School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Memory Books.
Cards, love letters, cat hat pin, calling cards, school event programs, and photographs (some silouetted) .
Designed b y E. V. Lodron Published by Barse and Hopkins New York, 1920.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.
Concerning baby John Clifford Shaw. Songs, Ads, Quotes, Havits and Training Tips.
Published ca. 1920 by The Toledo Metal Wheel Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.

Baby's hand; birth record; baby's presents; the christening; baby's family tree; important events; baby's first picture; 'Milk is baby's best friend'; facts about milk; a message on milk; recipes and care of milk; records of interest; baby's first birthday; baby's first christmas; baby's weight; weights and measures; baby's future; contributors to baby's bank account; baby welfare.

Pamphlet published b y The Lincoln Dairy Co., Hartford, Conn. 'Congratulations ... To the Mother and the New Baby are sent best wishes that life's richest blessings may shine upon them as the golden chains of affection draw them ever close through the years to come. And may the pages of this dainty brochure record events of joy and gladness; the round of happy days that are the sweetest years of life, the years of infancy.'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Category: Baby Books.
Concerning baby Shelia Romaine Stearns. Chapters: Quaint Thoughts; First Bank Account; Favorite Toys; Locks of Hair and on the mother's side ... Stools; Sore Buttocks; How to give an enema, etc.

Published by the Baby Mine Company, Grand Rapids, MI. Verse and Decorations by Melcena Burns Denny. 'Because they believe in Babies and love them; because they believe in Quality Advertising as well as quality products and service, you have the enjoyment of this unusual and helpful baby book -- their gift to you and Baby. And we believe that you, in turn, will want them to have the enjoyment of your telling them of your appreciation and of a share of your patronage.' Includes addresses of local Tampa Florida merchants.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entries of lost items

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labeled 1916 Norfolk, Virginia on front but has a much broader scope of contents: 1922 Sheet Music on inside cover; Lillian Russell clippings; Ben Turpin; 1911 Advertisement Apollo Player Piano; Several Dance Talley Cards; Post Cards (Mostly Virginia); Candy Wrappers; Several Vintage ACES from Playing Card Decks; Buster Keaton; Mary Pickford; Charlie Chaplin; Eddie Cantor Newspaper clipping; Hershey Candy Wrappers; Mayor Gaynor (NY) Being shot from newspaper photo; 1946 William Hart Obituary; The Old Copely House 1955; Vintage Automobiles; Howard Goshorn Comics; Will Rogers; W.C. Fields, more.
'In memoriam of my first suffrage parade 1912'
